title: New EPF Scheme 2026: Know withdrawal frequency limits for essential, housing and special needs
summary: Under the EPF Scheme, 2026, withdrawals have been simplified into three categories: essential needs, housing needs and special circumstances.

title: EPF Scheme 2026: Newly married members must update nomination, but spouse inclusion is not compulsory
summary: Under the EPF Scheme 2026, members must submit a fresh nomination after marriage, as earlier nominations become invalid. While nominating a spouse is optional, updating nominations ensures eligible family members receive EPF benefits without delays or legal complications.

title: 8th Pay Commission: Experts decode the fitment factor range and why allowances may not offset a lower multiplier
summary: The 8th Pay Commission is reviewing salaries, pensions and allowances for central government employees and pensioners, with the fitment factor remaining the key focus. Experts expect it to range between 1.90 and 2.86.

title: Kisan Vikas Patra interest rate for July-Sept quarter announced, investment to double in 115 months: Details here
summary: At the current interest rate of 7.5%, the invested amount doubles after 115 months, or 9 years and 7 months. Although the investment remains locked in until maturity, premature closure is allowed under specific conditions.

title: ₹14.65 lakh salary? Here's how you can legally reduce your income tax liability to zero under new tax regime
summary: A ₹14.65 lakh CTC does not necessarily mean you have to pay income tax under the new tax regime. With the right salary structure, employer contributions to EPF and NPS, and the standard deduction, your taxable income can be brought down to ₹11.99 lakh, making you eligible for zero tax liability.

title: Mutual funds holding cash: Is high cash balance a red flag for investors? Experts explain
summary: Experts suggest that cash holdings should be viewed as a portfolio management tool rather than a buy-or-sell signal. A fund's investment philosophy, portfolio quality, and long-term performance remain far more important than a single percentage shown in the monthly factsheet.
